state.next-action: The heavy-blob history cleanup is done and `main` pushes again. The shared provider contract is landed, `@portfolio/repub-builder` builds from the vendored standalone repo, hosted book-artifact and public-media publishers are live, `/apps` reads from seeded `site-app-records`, `/projects` now merge `project-records` metadata over repo-authored MDX bodies, `/resumes` now read `resume-records`, and planning-pack plus resume artifacts publish through `site-download-assets` with repo fallbacks where planned. Both the owner-only shell refresh in `global-auth-02` and the mounted Payload back office in `global-admin-01` are live, and Listen plus Reader runtime access now goes through shared Payload-backed repositories instead of route-level collection/query logic. Phase-1 site RAG excludes full book manuscript EPUB text by default (`global-data-dec-09`, `BOOKS-REQ-04`); `global-data-01-04` is closed in Global planning. Grime Time is now present in the seeded app catalog at `grimetime.app`, and the next portfolio delivery priority is clear: build `global-auth-03-03` as the final auth foundation slice, then finish the remaining `global-data-01-03` bootstrap handoff so the same core can move into Grime Time without reopening auth or storage architecture.

agent-id-policy.rule: Optional: register a unique id in STATE.xml before claiming tasks if you use CLI task-update.
